reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff in June 2025, Sudan has not ratified the AfCFTA. Sudan has been enveloped in a devastating and existential civil war between the SAF and RSF since April 2023, severely hampering the functions of the state. With no functional unified legislature to pass the agreement, and the Sovereignty Council focused on survival and military efforts, ratifying a continental trade agreement constitutes a very low priority. Given that there are merely 25 days between the knowledge cutoff (June 6, 2025) and the resolution date (July 1, 2025), and zero recent news indicating momentum toward ratification, the likelihood of this occurring in the short remaining window is exceptionally low.
